Load WJP Fonts

Source: R/utils.R
wjp_fonts.Rd

wjp_fonts() loads the standard fonts used in WJP visualizations from Google Fonts. This function should be called once at the beginning of your R session before creating any WJP charts.

Usage

wjp_fonts()

Value

No return value, called for side effects. Fonts are registered and showtext::showtext_auto() is enabled.

Details

The function loads the following font families:

  • Lato Full: Regular weight (400) and bold (700)

  • Lato Light: Light weight (300) and bold (700)

  • Lato Black: Black weight (900)

  • Inter Tight: Regular weight (400) and bold (700)

Examples

# Load fonts before creating charts
wjp_fonts()

# Now you can use the fonts in ggplot2
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Lato Full"))
